mnemonics: STEALTH TEXT: Secure Text Encoding Always Leaves Traces, Hiding Information Thoroughly.
keyPoints: Steganography conceals the existence of a message within text, unlike cryptography which scrambles it.
Common techniques include using whitespace (e.g., tabs/spaces), zero-width characters, and syntactic substitutions.
The key challenge is to alter the carrier text minimally to avoid detection while maximizing payload capacity.
commonMistakes: Assuming steganography provides encryption; it only provides secrecy of existence, not content security.
Altering the carrier text too much, making the hidden data obvious through unnatural formatting or word choice.
Forgetting that text steganography is often fragile and can be broken by copying/pasting or reformatting the text.
